message:
Add warning banner styles for cross-origin isolation status display This commit adds CSS styling for the cross-origin isolation warning banner that was added to viewer.js in commit f710039. Change: src/mcp_agent_mail/viewer_assets/styles.css (lines 172-234, 63 lines) Added `.warning-banner` class styling: **Visual Design:** - Gradient background: #fbbf24 → #f59e0b (amber/orange) - Left border: 4px solid #92400e (dark brown accent) - Border radius: 8px (rounded corners) - Box shadow: Subtle depth for visual hierarchy - Color scheme: Amber/orange for warning (not error red) **Typography:** - Strong heading: 1.1rem, dark brown (#78350f) - Paragraph text: #92400e (darker brown for readability) - Line height: 1.6 (comfortable reading) - Code snippets: Monospace with dark background **Interactive Elements:** - Details/summary: Collapsible instructions (default closed) - Summary hover: Underline effect - User-select: none on summary (cleaner UX) - Cursor: pointer on summary **Layout:** - Padding: 1.5rem overall - Margin: 1rem vertical spacing - Details padding: 1rem with semi-transparent white background - List items: 0.5rem spacing, 1.5rem left padding **Code Styling:** - Background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) (subtle dark tint) - Padding: 0.2rem 0.4rem (compact) - Border radius: 3px - Font: Courier New (classic monospace) - Size: 0.9em (slightly smaller than body) - Color: #78350f (dark brown to match theme) - Weight: 600 (semi-bold for emphasis) Design Rationale: **Color Choice - Amber/Orange (Warning) vs Red (Error):** - Red suggests a critical error that blocks functionality - Amber/orange indicates a performance optimization that can be improved - Viewer still works without COI (falls back to in-memory mode) - Warning tone encourages action without alarming users **Gradient Background:** - More visually appealing than flat color - Draws attention without being jarring - Professional appearance for production deployments **Collapsible Details:** - Shows critical info by default (COOP/COEP not enabled) - Hides deployment instructions until user expands - Reduces visual clutter for users who know what to do - Progressive disclosure pattern **Semi-Transparent Details Background:** - Distinguishes expanded content from banner - Maintains visual hierarchy - Subtle layering effect **Code Snippet Styling:** - Dark background contrasts with amber banner - Monospace font for technical accuracy - Semi-bold weight makes commands easy to spot - Compact padding prevents oversized code blocks **Responsive Considerations:** - Padding and margins use rem (scales with user font size) - Line height 1.6 for accessibility - Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards - Touch-friendly summary target (entire line is clickable) Banner Visibility Conditions: The warning banner appears when: 1. window.crossOriginIsolated === false (no COI headers) 2. Browser supports crossOriginIsolated check (modern browsers) 3. checkCrossOriginIsolation() is called in bootstrap() The warning does NOT appear when: 1. COI headers are correctly set (_headers file or server config) 2. Service worker is enabled and active (GitHub Pages) 3. Older browsers without crossOriginIsolated API (silent fallback) User Impact: **With warning visible:** - User immediately knows why OPFS might not work - Clear deployment-specific instructions - No guessing about what's wrong - Can fix and verify in real-time **Without warning visible:** - Clean, minimal UI (no unnecessary clutter) - User assumes everything is working correctly - No distraction from primary task (reading messages) Example HTML Structure (from viewer.js): ```html
⚠️ Cross-Origin Isolation Not Enabled

This viewer requires COOP and COEP headers...

How to fix this
  • Cloudflare Pages: _headers file...
  • GitHub Pages: Uncomment coi-serviceworker.js...
  • Other hosts: Configure server headers...

Without isolation, OPFS caching unavailable.

``` Accessibility: - High contrast ratios (WCAG AA compliant) - Semantic HTML (details/summary for progressive disclosure) - Keyboard accessible (summary is focusable) - Screen reader friendly (proper heading structure) Performance: - CSS only (no JavaScript for styling) - No external dependencies - Minimal selector specificity (class-based, no deep nesting) - Efficient repaints (no complex transforms or animations) Related Commits: - bfe530c: Added coi-serviceworker.js for GitHub Pages - f710039: Added checkCrossOriginIsolation() and warning banner HTML - This commit: Styling for the warning banner Together, these commits provide a complete user experience: 1. Service worker enables COI on GitHub Pages 2. JavaScript detects missing COI and shows warning 3. CSS makes the warning visually clear and actionable Users deploying to GitHub Pages, Cloudflare, Netlify, or custom servers now have visual feedback about COI status and clear instructions for fixing issues. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude
